rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly declared before use.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the actual types of expressions, e.g., bool for comparisons.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and constants are declared in the same annex or are visible in the component scope.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Validate that all expressions in guarantees and assumptions have well-defined types to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE logical operators include 'and', 'or', 'not(...)', and implication '=>'.
Constants in AGREE are declared with explicit types: const name : type = value;
Equations in AGREE should declare a type and use valid expressions: eq name : type = expression;
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-specific features in component type contracts.
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers that are not declared in the model or its dependencies.
